entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: Country in southern Africa where the study on input relief programs was conducted.

entities.name: SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: Region where drought and flood relief programs are common.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A period of deficient rainfall that triggers relief programs and affects food security.

entities.name: FLOOD
entities.description: An overflow of water that triggers relief programs.

entities.name: SEED
entities.description: Agricultural input distributed to farmers through relief programs.

entities.name: FERTILIZER
entities.description: Agricultural input distributed to farmers to improve crop yields.

entities.name: INPUT RELIEF PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs designed to distribute free seeds and fertilizers to affected households.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A chemical element found in fertilizer that contributes to food security.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient food, impacted by drought and fertilizer use.

entities.name: DIRECT HANDOUTS
entities.description: A distribution method where inputs are given directly to beneficiaries.

entities.name: SEED FAIRS
entities.description: A distribution method for seeds involving community fairs.

entities.name: VOUCHERS
entities.description: A distribution method where beneficiaries use vouchers at retail shops.

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Animals owned by households, used as a criterion for beneficiary selection.
